This upgrade, achieved through an Evolution kit, enhances overall performance, race condition consistency, and handling to increase driving satisfaction. The focus of the Evo package is on optimizing the synergy between aerodynamics and vehicle dynamics, particularly improving the interaction among tires, downforce balance, and electronic controls. Enhanced performance is delivered through increased downforce and new Pirelli tires. Early development stages included substantial work in Ferrari's GT driving simulator to refine the aerodynamic balance between the axles, aimed specifically at increasing front downforce to improve turn-in and decrease understeer exiting corners. The aerodynamic adjustments work in tandem with advanced control systems such as the E-Diff3, F1-TCS, and an evolved Side Slip Control (SSC) system that features new settings. These enhancements allow the control systems to share a common language, boosting cornering agility and exit traction, thanks to a revised estimation of the yaw angle adjusting for new performance levels. In collaboration with the Ferrari Styling Centre, the front end of the car underwent a comprehensive redesign, resulting in a 30% increase in aerodynamic efficiency compared to the standard 488 Challenge. Changes include a longer front overhang to augment downforce and optimize balance, extended radiator grille intakes, and a U-shaped intake at the bumper center to cool the front brakes and emphasize the car's sporty ethos. A large splitter with turning vanes at the bumper’s ends directs airflow more effectively, while modifications to the sides of the bumper facilitate a second side flick, enhancing airflow quality. As a result, the 488 Challenge Evo boasts 50% more downforce than the existing 488 Challenge, marking a significant leap in Ferrari's pursuit of race car perfection.

name: KC23 2023
description: The Ferrari KC23, a spectacular new entry in Ferrari's One-Off series, was recently unveiled as part of the brand's prestigious Special Projects programme. Commissioned by a prominent Ferrari collector, the KC23 is envisioned as a futuristic interpretation of closed-wheel racing. This unique vehicle, designed by the Ferrari Styling Centre under Flavio Manzoni's leadership, is based on the highly successful 488 GT3 Evo 2020, a car renowned for its dominance in global endurance racing since its debut in 2016. This one-off model encapsulates both elegance and exhilarating performance. When stationary, it exhibits a refined elegance, but in motion, it transforms, showcasing its groundbreaking aerodynamic features like motorized air intake vents and a dramatic rear wing. These elements not only enhance its visual appeal but significantly contribute to its aerodynamic efficiency. The KC23 inherits its layout, chassis, and engine from the 488 GT3 Evo 2020, ensuring that it remains deeply rooted in the rich racing heritage that has seen the 488 GT3 achieve over 530 race wins and more than 119 championships, making it the most successful racing Ferrari in the company's 76-year history. Designed exclusively for non-competitive track use, the KC23 features a dual configuration that highlights its sleek, sinuous form at rest, while its operational stance on the track is defined by the dynamic function of its air intakes and prominent rear wing. This combination of advanced engineering and aesthetic design showcases Ferrari's ongoing commitment to pushing the boundaries of automotive innovation and design.

name: F8 Tributo 2019-2023
description: The Ferrari F8 Tributo is a mid-rear-engined sports car that stands as the pinnacle of Ferrari's two-seater berlinetta design. Its name pays homage to the most powerful V8 engine in Ferrari's history, delivering 720 horsepower and a remarkable specific power output of 185 hp/l. This V8 engine, turbocharged for optimal performance, has been celebrated with numerous awards, including "Best Engine" for three consecutive years and the title of best engine of the last two decades in 2018. The F8 Tributo showcases exceptional handling and ride comfort, integrating advanced aerodynamics derived from Ferrari's extensive track experience. This model not only surpasses its predecessor, the 488 GTB, by delivering 50 more horsepower and being 40 kg lighter, but also boasts a 10% improvement in aerodynamic efficiency. The car's design by the Ferrari Styling Centre bridges traditional Ferrari aesthetics with new design language, emphasizing performance and aerodynamic efficiency. The vehicle dynamics of the F8 Tributo have been significantly enhanced. The integration of the Ferrari Dynamic Enhancer (FDE+) in the manettino's RACE position, the latest Side Slip Angle Control system (SSC 6.1), and a smaller diameter steering wheel improve the car's performance and accessibility. These systems ensure the car's power and handling are more intuitive and controllable, allowing drivers to push the car to its limits with greater confidence. Aerodynamics play a crucial role in the F8 Tributo's performance. The car is 10% more aerodynamically efficient than the 488 GTB, thanks to improvements in downforce without increasing drag. Innovations like the S-Duct at the front and the blown spoiler at the rear, coupled with advanced cooling systems, contribute to its superior performance. The rear design incorporates elements like the louvred rear screen, reminiscent of the iconic F40, and a larger spoiler that enhances both style and functionality. Inside, the F8 Tributo retains Ferrari's classic driver-focused cockpit with a modern twist. The dashboard, door panels, and tunnel are all newly designed, featuring a new generation steering wheel and controls. High-quality materials and options like carbon-fibre accents and a 7" touchscreen display for the passenger add to the luxurious yet sporty feel of the cabin. The overall design aims to enhance the sense of lightness and streamlined functionality, maintaining Ferrari's commitment to driver engagement and comfort.

name: F8 Spider 2020-2023
description: The Ferrari F8 Spider is the latest addition to Ferrari's illustrious line of drop-top sports cars, featuring the most successful mid-rear-mounted V8 in history. Designed alongside the F8 Tributo berlinetta, it utilizes Ferrari's compact and efficient Retractable Hard Top (RHT), which defines the car's streamlined profile. This new model continues the tradition of Ferrari's open-top V8s that began with the 308 GTS in 1977, balancing sportiness with usability and setting a new benchmark for performance and handling in its class. The F8 Spider features the celebrated V8 engine, a development of the engine that has won the "International Engine of the Year Award" for four consecutive years. This 3902 cc unit delivers 720 horsepower at 8,000 rpm, making it the most powerful V8 Ferrari has produced for a non-special series model. The engine's instant power delivery, zero turbo lag, and unique exhaust note are complemented by advanced aerodynamic solutions derived from Ferrari's track experience. The design of the F8 Spider incorporates several aerodynamic enhancements, including a revised front end with an S-Duct and new horizontal LED headlights. The rear spoiler has been redesigned to be larger, enhancing downforce without increasing drag, and features the classic twin light cluster and body-colored tail. The engine cover is distinctively styled with a central spine and sculpted strakes that aid in heat dissipation, inspired by Ferrari's F1 design elements. Inside, the F8 Spider maintains a driver-focused cockpit with a new generation steering wheel and sporty seats. The dashboard features an aluminum sail panel and optional carbon-fiber accents, with a central 7" touchscreen for the passenger. The layout is designed to enhance the sense of lightness and streamline the interior, creating a cohesive and engaging driving environment. In terms of performance, the F8 Spider surpasses its predecessor, the 488 Spider, with a 50 horsepower increase and a 20 kg weight reduction. The car is also 20 kg heavier than the more extreme 488 Pista Spider but features a 10% improvement in overall efficiency. Advanced vehicle dynamics systems, including the latest Ferrari Dynamic Enhancer Plus (FDE+) and Side Slip Angle Control (SSC 6.1), make the car's impressive performance more accessible and easier to control for a broader range of drivers. Overall, the Ferrari F8 Spider blends cutting-edge performance with elegant design, setting a new standard in the realm of convertible sports cars.

name: SP48 Unica 2022
description: The Ferrari SP48 Unica, the newest addition to Ferrari's exclusive One-Off series, exemplifies bespoke automotive craftsmanship, tailored precisely to the specifications of a single client. Built on the F8 Tributo platform, this unique two-seater sports berlinetta showcases an aggressive and distinctive design, characterized by its arrow-shaped front profile and meticulously redesigned headlights and brake air intakes. The project, directed by Chief Design Officer Flavio Manzoni, uses advanced procedural-parametric modelling and 3D prototyping to achieve a seamless and dynamic aesthetic. Key to the SP48 Unica's design are the innovative 3D grilles and procedural graphic solutions that create a striking transition from the black elements, including the windows, roof, and engine cover, to the body color. This design choice emphasizes the car's sculpted appearance, further enhanced by the smaller side windows and the absence of a rear screen. The central roof section features air intakes set into the carbon-fiber engine cover, contributing to the car's aerodynamic efficiency and emphasizing its sophisticated styling. Aerodynamically, the SP48 Unica departs significantly from the F8 Tributo. The redesigned air intakes on the front bumper and under the rear spoiler, combined with an intercooler intake behind the side windows, optimize cooling and improve aerodynamic balance. The extended rear overhang reduces suction from the roof area, increasing rear downforce and enhancing the car's performance. Inside, the SP48 Unica retains the technical layout of the F8 Tributo but features a distinctive combination of colors and trims that reflect its sleek and sporty exterior. Specially developed black laser-perforated Alcantara® on the seats and cabin trim reveals glimpses of iridescent reddish-orange fabric, matching the exterior color and integrating the hexagonal motif from the grilles. Matte carbon-fiber elements and Grigio Canna di Fucile accents add a sense of technical sophistication and exclusivity. The Ferrari SP48 Unica, designed for a dedicated client deeply involved in its creation, represents a bold reinterpretation of a sports car that emphasizes Ferrari's core values of innovation and passion. It masterfully transforms the base model, enhancing its racing soul and dedication to speed, resulting in a bespoke masterpiece that is a testament to Ferrari's commitment to personalized automotive excellence.

name: SP-8 2023
description: The Ferrari SP-8, the latest addition to Ferrari's One-Off series, has been unveiled as a part of the marque's Special Projects programme. Designed by the Ferrari Styling Centre under Flavio Manzoni, this unique mid-rear-engined V8 roadster is based on the F8 Spider, inheriting its layout, chassis, and engine. The SP-8 joins Ferrari's most exclusive segment, where each car is uniquely tailored to individual client specifications, representing the peak of Ferrari's customization capabilities. The SP-8 celebrates its 3.9-litre V8 twin-turbo engine, renowned for its performance and having won the prestigious Engine of the Year award four times. The number 8 holds cultural significance for the client from Taiwan, symbolizing success and good fortune in Chinese culture. The most distinctive feature of the SP-8 is its roofless design, creating a pure two-seater roadster experience. Extensive aerodynamic refinements, including CFD simulations, wind tunnel, and track testing, ensure the car maintains acoustic comfort and wind dynamics comparable to its inspiration, the F8 Spider. The design theme of the SP-8 focuses on the seamless fusion of its volumes. The unpainted carbon-fiber front blends into the tail, creating a striking two-tone effect. The removal of the retractable hard top allowed for a complete restyling of the tail section, resulting in a full volume with a taut belt line. The car's volume is split into two parts connected by a functional central matte black area, which includes the side air intakes and engine ducts. The upper section of this band features a vent with longitudinal elements reminiscent of classic Ferrari designs. At the front, the SP-8 features an imposing full-width cast aluminum grille made from a single 3D-printed mold, with vertical strakes optimized for air flow to the front radiators. The collaboration between the Ferrari Styling Centre and the aerodynamics team was crucial in perfecting these elements. Other redesigned features include the headlights with special masks and lenses, Roma-derived rear lights with specific lenses, the windscreen, tailpipes similar to the 296 GTB, and custom-designed wheel rims in matte Grigio NART. These five-spoke wheels are a modern take on classic Ferrari rims, designed for reduced weight and enhanced aesthetics. Inside, the SP-8's central console has been modified to house the F1 gearbox commands, adapting the iconic shift-gate for this application. The interior is finished with laser-etched Navy Blue Alcantara® seats, gradient effect cloth, and twill fabric carpets with an iridescent effect. The exterior color, matte Argento Micalizzato, pairs brilliantly with the carbon-fiber section in glossy iridescent Blue Sandstone, while the Blu Scuro Stellato color used to link the two bodywork sections was specifically developed for this car. The Ferrari SP-8 is a bespoke masterpiece designed for a client deeply involved in its creation, epitomizing Ferrari's dedication to innovation, customization, and the pure driving experience.

name: HC25 2026
description: The Ferrari HC25 is a unique one-off model created under Ferrari’s Special Projects programme. Unveiled at the Ferrari Racing Days event at the Circuit of the Americas, it is based on the F8 Spider platform and retains its mid-rear engine layout, chassis, and 3.9-litre twin-turbo V8 powertrain. This makes the HC25 the final non-hybrid mid-engine V8 Spider from Ferrari. Designed by the Ferrari Design Studio under the direction of Flavio Manzoni, it serves as a bridge between the brand’s classic mid-engine spiders and its more modern flagship models like the 12Cilindri and F80. The styling takes a bolder, more contemporary approach while keeping the voluptuous and sensual character typical of Ferrari’s mid-engine cars. It features pure, clean surfaces with vertical flanks defined by sharp crests and geometric lines. The car expresses muscular forms around the wheels and creates a strong graphic identity through its dual-volume design, where the front and rear sections appear as distinct bodies connected by a prominent central band. This glossy black band runs along the side of the car, incorporating air intakes and heat extraction for the powertrain. It creates an arrow-shaped flow that starts at the rear wheels, sweeps up over the door, and integrates the handle into a solid aluminium blade. The HC25 has refined proportions with reduced glazing and a lower shoulder line for a more planted look. At the front, it features all-new slim headlamps with vertical boomerang-style DRLs that echo the split rear lighting design. Finished in matte Moonlight Grey with contrasting glossy black elements and yellow accents on the logos, brake calipers, and interior details, the car rides on distinctive five-spoke wheels. It produces 720 cv (710 hp) at 7,000 rpm and 770 Nm of torque at 3,250 rpm. Performance includes 0-100 km/h in 2.9 seconds, 0-200 km/h in 8.2 seconds, and a top speed of 340 km/h.

name: SF90 Stradale 2020
description: Ferrari has launched the SF90 Stradale, its first series production Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV), signaling a new era in its storied history. The SF90 Stradale embodies a shift in automotive paradigms, delivering performance that sets a new benchmark for production cars with 1,000 horsepower and a weight-to-power ratio of 1.57 kg/hp. This model introduces a V8 as Ferrari's top engine offering for the first time. The vehicle's name commemorates the 90th anniversary of Scuderia Ferrari, reflecting the deep connection between Ferrari’s track and road vehicles. The SF90 Stradale showcases the pinnacle of technology from Maranello, translating Ferrari's competitive edge directly into its road cars. It features a 90° V8 turbo engine, the most powerful 8-cylinder engine in Ferrari's history, complemented by three electric motors that add up to 220 horsepower, providing a total output of 1,000 horsepower. This hybrid system does not complicate the driving experience; rather, it simplifies it through four selectable power unit modes managed by sophisticated control systems. The SF90 Stradale is Ferrari’s first sports car with 4WD, necessary to manage the substantial power, achieving 0-100 km/h in just 2.5 seconds and 0-200 km/h in 6.7 seconds. Dynamic control advancements include the RAC-e (electronic cornering set-up regulator), which uses two front motors to extend torque vectoring, enhancing handling and simplifying limit driving. The new chassis and bodyshell are constructed using multi-material technology, including carbon fiber, optimized for maximum performance, rigidity, and a low center of gravity. Innovative aerodynamics were developed to handle increased thermal energy from the power unit, featuring an active shut-off Gurney system for dynamic airflow adjustment, optimizing aerodynamics across various driving conditions. Stylistically, the SF90 Stradale departs significantly from the traditional mid-rear-engined sports berlinetta, drawing inspiration from Ferrari's latest supercars. The cockpit's advanced ergonomics are influenced by a track-focused design philosophy, emphasizing a seamless interaction between driver and vehicle. The interior includes a 16-inch curved HD screen fully controllable from a new steering wheel integrated with touchpad and haptic buttons. The SF90 Stradale introduces a new ignition key with keyless technology, incorporating into the car's design a nod to Ferrari's heritage with grille-style automatic gearbox controls. The standard model can be upgraded to the Assetto Fiorano specification, featuring GT racing-derived enhancements and lightweight materials for improved track performance, including a carbon-fibre rear spoiler generating 390 kg of downforce at 250 km/h and specialized Michelin tires.

name: SF90 Spider 2021
description: Ferrari introduced the SF90 Spider, the spider version of the SF90 Stradale, through a dedicated digital event that allowed global enthusiasts to explore the car's advanced features and development insights. This model is Ferrari’s first production plug-in hybrid spider, setting new standards in performance and innovation within Ferrari's lineup and the broader sports car segment. The SF90 Spider maintains the same high specifications and performance records as the SF90 Stradale but enhances the driving experience with its convertible design, featuring Ferrari’s latest retractable hard top (RHT) technology. This RHT is not only compact, light, and quick to operate (deployable in just 14 seconds while driving), but it also ensures better noise insulation and element protection. Constructed from aluminum, the RHT is about 40 kg lighter than traditional hard tops and uses less space, enhancing both performance and comfort. The convertible also offers an Assetto Fiorano package for those seeking to maximize their track experience. This package includes Multimatic shock absorbers from Ferrari’s GT racing, high-performance materials like carbon-fibre and titanium reducing the weight by 21 kg, a carbon-fibre rear spoiler, and specially designed Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires that enhance dry track performance due to their softer compound and reduced grooving. The package can be visually distinguished by an optional two-tone livery, emphasizing the car’s racing pedigree.

name: SF90 XX Stradale 2024
description: Ferrari introduces the SF90 Stradale, its first series production Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV), marking a significant milestone in the company's history. The SF90 Stradale showcases a paradigm shift in performance for production cars with specifications like 1,000 horsepower, a weight-to-power ratio of 1.57 kg/hp, and 390 kg of downforce at 250 km/h. This makes the V8 the most powerful range-topping model in Ferrari’s lineup for the first time. The SF90 Stradale's name reflects its performance achievements and the 90th anniversary of Scuderia Ferrari, illustrating the synergy between Ferrari’s track and road cars. It is powered by a 90° V8 turbo engine with an output of 780 horsepower, the highest for any 8-cylinder in Ferrari history. This is complemented by three electric motors, adding 220 horsepower, integrated seamlessly to enhance the driving experience without adding complexity. This model also introduces 4WD, enabling it to exploit its hybrid powertrain's potential fully, with remarkable acceleration times of 0-100 km/h in 2.5 seconds and 0-200 km/h in 6.7 seconds. The SF90 Stradale includes the RAC-e (electronic cornering set-up regulator), which uses two front electric motors for enhanced torque vectoring, simplifying driving at the limit. The vehicle features a new multi-material chassis and bodyshell, including carbon fibre, optimized for weight, rigidity, and a low centre of gravity. Aerodynamically, it incorporates innovative solutions like the shut-off Gurney, an active system that adjusts airflow to balance drag and downforce effectively. Stylistically, the SF90 Stradale departs from traditional designs, drawing from recent Ferrari supercars, and introduces a digital cockpit with a 16" curved HD screen, fully operable from a newly designed steering wheel. The interior merges past and present with grille-style automatic gearbox controls reminiscent of Ferrari's manual gear-shift gate. The introduction of a new ignition key with keyless technology and optional two-tone livery underlines Ferrari’s blend of tradition and innovation. Customers can choose the standard model or the Assetto Fiorano variant, which features racing-derived enhancements for a lighter, more track-focused performance. This includes Multimatic shock absorbers, lightweight materials like carbon fibre and titanium, and specialized Michelin Pilot Sport Cup2 tires for optimal track performance.

name: SF90 XX Spider 2024
description: Ferrari has unveiled two new limited series models, the SF90 XX Stradale and its counterpart, the SF90 XX Spider, with production limited to 799 and 599 units respectively. These V8-powered plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) embody Ferrari's most extreme special versions, pushing the performance of their road-going models to unprecedented levels, drawing on the legacy of past benchmarks like the 488 Pista and 812 Competizione. The SF90 XX Stradale is based on the SF90 Stradale and enhances its track and on-the-limit capabilities significantly. It boasts an increased output of 1,030 horsepower, 30 more than the SF90 Stradale, through specific software adjustments and radical aerodynamics including a fixed rear spoiler—the first on a road-going Ferrari since the F50—producing 530 kg of downforce at 250 km/h. The SF90 XX Spider merges top performance with the pleasure of open-air driving, featuring advanced aerodynamics and a Ferrari Retractable Hard Top (RHT) that deploys in 14 seconds and can operate up to speeds of 45 km/h. Both models share a PHEV layout with a V8 engine integrated with three electric motors delivering a combined 1,030 horsepower. Significant engineering improvements include polished inlet and exhaust ducts, new pistons, and specific machining of the combustion chamber, enhancing efficiency and reducing the engine weight by 3.5 kg. The SF90 XX Stradale's acoustic design has also been refined, optimizing the hot tube system to transmit engine sounds into the cabin, enhancing the Ferrari V8 sound experience. The electric motors contribute an additional 233 horsepower, supported by a high-performance lithium-ion battery that offers a 25 km range in full-electric mode, with a maximum speed of 135 km/h when powered solely by the front motors. Both models retain the 8-speed dual-clutch gearbox seen in the SF90 Stradale, but with a new gear-shift logic for more engaging acceleration and improved gear-shift sounds. Aerodynamically, the SF90 XX Stradale sets a new standard with double the downforce of its predecessor, achieving significant gains in grip and lap times. The innovative rear wing plays a critical role in this aerodynamic enhancement, shaped to optimize air flow interactions around the car. The SF90 XX Stradale also introduces new cooling systems and aerodynamic adjustments to manage increased power and downforce effectively. This latest release from Ferrari not only highlights their continuous innovation in hybrid technology but also cements the SF90 XX models as pinnacle achievements in combining race-derived technology with road-going capabilities, embodying the ultimate in performance, style, and exclusivity.

name: 849 Testarossa 2026
description: On September 9, 2025, Ferrari unveiled the 849 Testarossa, a groundbreaking plug-in hybrid supercar that serves as the direct successor to the SF90 Stradale, reviving one of the brand's most iconic names from the 1980s while embracing cutting-edge electrification. Named to honor the original Testarossa's "red head" heritage—referencing the scarlet cam covers of Ferrari's 1950s racing legends—the "849" denotes its eight-cylinder V8 configuration and 49 cc displacement per cylinder. This mid-engine berlinetta combines a revamped twin-turbo 4.0-liter V8 producing 818 horsepower with three electric motors (two up front for all-wheel drive and one integrated into the rear eight-speed dual-clutch transmission) delivering an additional 218 hp, for a combined output of 1,036 horsepower and 664 lb-ft of torque. Powered by a 6.5-kWh battery, it offers up to 16 miles of electric-only range at speeds reaching 81 mph, blending zero-emission capability with Ferrari's signature visceral performance. The 849 Testarossa's design, crafted under Ferrari's Styling Centre, draws subtle inspiration from its storied predecessor without overt retro flourishes, instead evolving the angular, futuristic aesthetics introduced in the F80 supercar. Its low-slung silhouette features sharper LED headlights, expansive air intakes for enhanced cooling (15% more than the SF90), and a sculpted rear with quad exhausts and a subtle active spoiler generating 915 pounds of downforce at 155 mph—25 kg more than its forebear. The chassis, constructed from lightweight aluminum and carbon fiber, maintains the SF90's dry weight of 3,461 pounds through strategic component reductions, including lighter pistons and turbochargers, ensuring razor-sharp handling. Aerodynamic refinements and a revised suspension with active dampers prioritize both track precision and road composure, making it a versatile weapon for demanding drivers. Performance metrics underscore the 849 Testarossa's supremacy: it surges from 0 to 62 mph in under 2.3 seconds, achieves a top speed exceeding 205 mph, and laps Ferrari's Fiorano circuit in 1:17.5—a full 1.5 seconds quicker than the SF90 Stradale. Advanced electronics elevate the experience, including the new Ferrari Integrated Vehicle Estimator (FIVE), a digital twin that optimizes traction control, ABS Evo for cornering ABS, and Side Slip Control 6.0 for drift mastery. The hybrid system enables seamless torque vectoring and on-demand AWD, while a revised gear-shift strategy amplifies exhaust notes during upshifts, creating an immersive cockpit symphony. This fusion of raw power and intelligent tech positions the 849 as Ferrari's new halo car, outpacing rivals like the Lamborghini Revuelto in outright velocity and efficiency. The interior channels a driver-centric ethos with a wraparound "sail" dashboard in Alcantara and carbon fiber, a redesigned multifunction steering wheel with eManettino dials, and a high-resolution HMI supporting wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. Ample personalization options via Ferrari's Tailor Made program allow bespoke leathers, paints, and accents, while the flying-bridge center console enhances ergonomics without sacrificing the analog thrill of physical controls. Priced from €460,000 (approximately $540,000) in Europe, with U.S. deliveries slated for mid-2026 at a premium due to tariffs, the 849 Testarossa coupe embodies Maranello's forward vision—honoring its past while redefining hybrid hypercar excellence for a new era.

name: 849 Testarossa Spider 2026
description: Unveiled alongside its coupe sibling on September 9, 2025, the Ferrari 849 Testarossa Spider elevates the plug-in hybrid formula with open-air exhilaration, replacing the SF90 Spider as Ferrari's pinnacle convertible while perpetuating the legendary Testarossa lineage. Sharing the coupe's nomenclature—"849" signifying its V8 roots and per-cylinder displacement—the Spider deploys the identical powertrain: a potent twin-turbo 4.0-liter V8 yielding 818 hp augmented by three electric motors for 218 hp more, totaling 1,036 horsepower and 664 lb-ft. The 6.5-kWh battery enables 16 miles of EV mode up to 81 mph, but the Spider's retractable hardtop (RHT) mechanism—deploying in 14 seconds at speeds up to 28 mph—transforms it into a sensory masterpiece, amplifying the hybrid's guttural soundtrack and turbo spool en plein air. This open-top berlinetta, weighing just 198 pounds more than the coupe at 3,659 pounds dry, reaffirms Ferrari's mastery in balancing indulgence with uncompromised dynamics. Design-wise, the 849 Testarossa Spider mirrors the coupe's sleek, F80-inspired contours but adapts them for convertibility, featuring a lightweight composite RHT that folds neatly into a sculpted rear deck to preserve the low roofline when stowed. Aerodynamic tweaks include additional chassis reinforcements (adding only 22 pounds) and a powered glass wind deflector to minimize turbulence, ensuring the cabin remains a cocoon of calm at triple-digit speeds. Prominent cooling vents and larger intercoolers handle the 15% increased thermal demands, while the active rear wing boosts downforce to 915 pounds at 155 mph—identical to the closed-roof variant. Subtle cues like the quad taillights and flared arches evoke the original Testarossa's wide-hip drama, but rendered in a modern, wind-cheating form that prioritizes both beauty and brutality. On the performance front, the Spider inherits the coupe's benchmark figures with negligible compromise: 0-62 mph in under 2.3 seconds, a top speed over 205 mph, and a Fiorano lap time shaved to 1:17.5 thanks to the same FIVE digital estimator, ABS Evo, and Side Slip Control 6.0 suite. The hybrid architecture's torque vectoring shines brighter with the top down, delivering instantaneous AWD grip for canyon carving or track assaults, while the revised upshift acoustics—borrowed from the SF90 XX Spider—create an even more visceral exhaust ballet. Despite the added mass, Ferrari engineers tuned the suspension for heightened feedback at the limit, ensuring the Spider thrills without sacrificing the ride refinement that defines grand touring convertibles. It's a machine that democratizes hypercar pace for al fresco adventures, outshining peers in blending raw speed with romantic allure. The Spider's cockpit echoes the coupe's angular minimalism, with a C-shaped dashboard wrapping occupants in premium Alcantara, carbon-Kevlar accents, and intuitive controls via the eManettino and digital displays. The RHT maintains acoustic isolation comparable to the fixed-roof model, and a deployable wind blocker enhances open-top usability, while vast Tailor Made customization lets owners curate everything from quilted leathers to exposed carbon weaves. Starting at €500,000 (around $586,000) in Europe, with U.S. arrivals targeted for late 2026 and higher pricing anticipated, the 849 Testarossa Spider captures Ferrari's dual soul—heritage-fueled innovation that invites drivers to savor every roar, rev, and rush under the open sky.

name: 296 GTB 2022
description: The Ferrari 296 GTB was unveiled during an online event, marking a significant evolution in Maranello’s lineup with its new mid-rear-engined two-seater berlinetta setup. The 296 GTB introduces a groundbreaking 663 horsepower 120° V6 engine, paired with an electric motor that adds another 167 horsepower, culminating in a total output of 830 horsepower. This setup not only highlights the car’s robust performance but also represents Ferrari’s first 6-cylinder engine in a road car bearing the Prancing Horse badge, offering a unique and exhilarating sound. The nomenclature of the 296 GTB reflects its engine displacement and cylinder count, aligning with Ferrari’s tradition of emphasizing significant engine developments. This new model harks back to Ferrari’s extensive motorsport heritage, drawing parallels to its historical V6 engines used in models like the 246 SP and the Formula 1 156 F1. The 296 GTB is designed not only for peak performance but also for daily driving convenience, featuring a plug-in hybrid (PHEV) system that provides 25km of all-electric range and instantaneous pedal response. Its compact dimensions and advanced dynamic control systems ensure remarkable agility and responsiveness, further complemented by a design that nods to historical models like the 1963 250 LM. For those seeking to maximize their track experience, the 296 GTB can be equipped with the Assetto Fiorano package, which includes lightweight materials and aerodynamic enhancements to exploit the car's full performance potential.

name: Assetto Fiorano 2022
description: The Ferrari 296 GTB, noted for introducing the first 120-degree V6 in Ferrari's production lineup, offers a specialized upgrade in the form of the Assetto Fiorano package. This package enhances the berlinetta's performance through various GT racing-derived modifications. It includes Multimac shock absorbers optimized for track performance, high downforce carbon fiber front bumper appendages, a lightweight Lexan rear screen, and extensive use of carbon fiber throughout the vehicle's interior and exterior. More than merely adding components, the Assetto Fiorano redesigns crucial elements of the 296 GTB, such as the door panel, which reduces weight by over 12 kg, and incorporates a LaFerrari-inspired active rear bumper that significantly increases downforce by up to 360 kg. These modifications enable the car to maximize the performance of its 819-hp 3.0L 120-degree V6 engine. For those interested in customization, the Assetto Fiorano offers extensive options including 26 exterior colors, 7 wheel designs, 5 brake caliper configurations, and exclusive liveries in Giallo Modena or Argento Nurburgring. Customers can personalize their Ferrari either in person at showrooms or via remote design studios, with dealers like Ferrari Lake Forest providing comprehensive support for configuring these exceptional vehicles.

name: 296 GTS 2023
description: Ferrari unveiled the 296 GTS, a new evolution of Maranello's mid-rear-engined two-seater berlinetta spider, during an online event. This model extends the excitement and performance of the 296 GTB to a convertible format, redefining enjoyment in both high-performance and everyday driving contexts. The 296 GTS features the same 663 horsepower, 120° V6 engine found in the 296 GTB, combined with an electric motor that delivers an additional 167 horsepower. This combination results in a total power output of 830 horsepower, making it the first 6-cylinder spider with the Prancing Horse badge to offer such performance levels. The experience is amplified by the unique and exhilarating engine sound, particularly enjoyable with the top down. Echoing Ferrari's longstanding naming conventions, the GTS in the model’s name stands for Gran Turismo Spider, highlighting the new engine's significance and the ushering in of a new V6 era that draws from Ferrari's 75-year legacy in motorsport and performance engineering. The 296 GTS also incorporates a plug-in hybrid system that provides zero-delay pedal response and a 25km all-electric driving range, enhancing its usability. The car's agility and responsiveness are supported by its compact dimensions, innovative dynamic control systems, and finely tuned aerodynamics. Visually, the 296 GTS maintains a sporty and streamlined appearance, whether the retractable hard top (RHT) is deployed or retracted. The RHT can be operated in just 14 seconds at speeds up to 45 km/h, folding neatly without disrupting the car's thermal dynamics or overall design balance. The design allows for a visible engine bay through a rear window when the top is down, enhancing the visual appeal and engaging driving experience. For those looking to maximize their track performance, Ferrari offers the Assetto Fiorano package, which includes specialized lightweight features and aerodynamic enhancements, further enhancing the 296 GTS's capabilities.

name: 296 GT3 2023
description: The Ferrari 296 GT3, an innovative evolution from the 296 GTB, signifies Ferrari's future in GT racing, emphasizing technology transfer from the track to production cars. Developed to maintain Ferrari's successful legacy in motorsports dating back to 1949, the 296 GT3 embodies a new design philosophy, optimized for modern GT racing regulations. Diverging from the hybrid system of the 296 GTB, the 296 GT3 features a turbocharged 120° V6 engine without the electric unit, optimizing it for the unique demands of GT racing. This engine arrangement offers significant advantages in terms of compactness, a lower center of gravity, and reduced mass, enhancing the car's power and agility. It is positioned lower and more forward than in the road car, improving both the center of gravity and torsional rigidity by approximately 10% compared to the previous 488 GT3. The car's design integrates feedback from both professional and gentleman drivers, focusing on performance, reliability, and drivability across various racing formats. Engineers collaborated closely with partners to refine the engine’s internal fluid dynamics and the specific track components, aiming to maximize performance while significantly reducing fuel consumption for strategic flexibility in races. A newly designed gearbox, configured with a single-disc clutch and six transversely arranged gears, complements the engine's layout. This setup enhances the car's aerodynamics and weight distribution, with electronic clutch actuation managed from the steering wheel for rapid gear changes. The 296 GT3 also showcases a sophisticated aerodynamic package, achieving a 20% increase in downforce over its predecessor. The car's aerodynamics are meticulously tuned to optimize performance and stability, making it accessible and competitive for drivers of all levels. The bodywork and wing elements are carefully designed to interact aerodynamically, optimizing airflow and performance in various racing conditions, including drafting scenarios. Inside, the cockpit has been entirely redesigned for enhanced ergonomics, visibility, and accessibility, crucial for endurance racing. The adjustments include a more intuitive layout, better ventilation, and customizable seating and pedal positions to accommodate drivers of different statures, enhancing comfort over long stints. The chassis of the 296 GT3, entirely new and made from aluminum, offers a combination of lightness and strength, with a longer wheelbase and a redesigned suspension system compared to the 488 GT3. This design supports optimal tire performance and longevity, crucial for the rigors of GT racing. In summary, the Ferrari 296 GT3 is a blend of advanced engineering, driver-focused ergonomics, and effective aerodynamics, tailored to excel in both professional and amateur GT racing circuits. It represents a strategic evolution of Ferrari's racing philosophy, ensuring competitiveness and enjoyment in the demanding arena of GT racing.

name: 296 Challenge 2024
description: Ferrari has unveiled the 296 Challenge, the latest addition to its storied Ferrari Challenge series, set to debut in the 2024 season. This marks the ninth model introduced by Ferrari for its single-make championship, now in its 32nd season, presented at the Finali Mondiali at Mugello Circuit. The 296 Challenge represents a significant evolution from its road-going counterpart, the 296 GTB, drawing extensively on the technological and design advancements of the 296 GT3 race car. This model is specifically tailored for both seasoned and aspiring GT racers, featuring extensive modifications that enhance its suitability for track use while moving away from the hybrid powertrain of the 296 GTB to focus on pure combustion performance. With a 700 cv output from its V6 twin-turbo engine, the 296 Challenge achieves a record specific power output for its class of 234 cv/l, devoid of the hybrid components. This significant increase in power is complemented by a reduction in weight, enhancing both speed and agility. Aerodynamically, the car incorporates advanced elements like the S-Duct and a swan-neck rear wing from the 296 GT3, pushing downforce capabilities to new extremes. The car's braking system has also seen major improvements with the introduction of CCM-R PLUS discs, providing enhanced durability and performance derived from Formula 1 technology. These changes collectively enable the 296 Challenge to lap Mugello two seconds faster than its predecessor, the 488 Challenge Evo, while maintaining performance consistency throughout a race. The removal of the hybrid system has led to other significant modifications including a new linear exhaust system that reduces back pressure by 30%, boosting engine performance. The cooling system layout has been adapted to these changes, optimizing airflow for both cooling and aerodynamic efficiency. The 296 Challenge's cockpit is designed for both performance and comfort, featuring a revised steering wheel layout, enhanced ergonomics, and an updated control layout for easy accessibility during races. Safety is paramount, with the roll cage meticulously designed to meet FIA standards, and the vehicle's structure engineered to maximize both lightness and strength. Overall, the 296 Challenge not only continues Ferrari's legacy in providing cutting-edge race cars for its one-make series but also sets a new benchmark in track performance, marrying Ferrari's road car heritage with its racing prowess. This model is a clear illustration of technology transfer from GT racing to a format accessible to Ferrari's sporting clients, promising thrilling on-track performance and a comprehensive racing experience.

name: 296 Speciale 2025
description: Ferrari has unveiled the 296 Speciale, a mid-rear engined plug-in hybrid berlinetta, joining the elite lineage of special version Ferrari berlinettas like the 458 Speciale and 488 Pista. Designed to deliver unmatched driving thrills, it sets a new benchmark for agility, responsiveness, and stability in its segment. The 296 Speciale amplifies the 296 GTB’s performance with a plug-in hybrid powertrain, featuring a 120° V6 twin-turbo engine and an electric motor, producing a record-breaking 880 hp for a rear-wheel-drive Ferrari. Drawing from motorsport, the engine incorporates titanium connecting rods, reinforced pistons, and a Formula 1-derived knock control system, boosting the V6 to 700 cv. The electric motor delivers 180 cv in extra boost mode, enhancing the eight-speed DCT for faster shifts. Aerodynamic innovations, including an aero damper, vertical fins, and an active rear spoiler with a new Medium Downforce setting, generate 435 kg of downforce at 250 km/h, 20% more than the 296 GTB. Weight is reduced by 60 kg using carbon fiber and titanium, achieving a 1.60 kg/cv power-to-weight ratio. Enhanced ABS Evo, revised suspension, and lower ride height improve handling and braking precision. The 296 Speciale’s V6 retains its distinctive sound, enriched by optimized acoustics and new ducts, delivering an exhilarating experience. Tailored for enthusiasts seeking ultimate performance, it redefines driving engagement in Ferrari’s production range.

name: 296 Speciale Piloti 2025
description: Ferrari unveiled the 296 Speciale Piloti Ferrari, a Tailor Made configuration, on June 13, 2025, at Circuit de la Sarthe, ahead of the 24 Hours of Le Mans. This exclusive model honors Ferrari’s FIA World Endurance Championship (WEC) successes and its client-racing drivers, drawing inspiration from the 499P Hypercar, which won Le Mans in 2023 and 2024. Reserved for clients involved in Ferrari’s competitive or non-competitive motorsport activities, it reflects the brand’s racing legacy. The car features a livery in four racing-inspired colors—Rosso Scuderia, Blu Tour De France, Nero Daytona, and Argento Nürburgring—with Giallo Modena accents, a hand-painted WEC logo, an Italian flag on the front bumper, and a customizable race number. The display model bore number 51, commemorating the 2023 Le Mans victory by Alessandro Pier Guidi, James Calado, and Antonio Giovinazzi. The interior includes black Alcantara racing seats with fireproof fabric inserts matching official driver suits, carbon fiber elements displaying the chosen race number, and customizable metal footwell surfaces. A carbon identification plaque and personalized carbon-fiber door sill enhance the bespoke design. While mechanically identical to the 296 Speciale, it features a plug-in hybrid powertrain with a 120-degree twin-turbo V6 and electric motor, delivering 880 cv. Compared to the 296 GTB, it has 20% more downforce, a lighter build, and a revised chassis for sharper track handling, incorporating rear gamma wings, suspended splitters, and an enlarged diffuser. This configuration, exclusive to Ferrari’s racing clientele, celebrates track-focused performance and personalization.

name: 296 GT3 EVO 2026
description: Ferrari unveiled the 296 GT3 Evo at the 24 Hours of Spa-Francorchamps in 2025, three years after the 296 GT3 debuted at the same event. The Evo builds on the 296 GT3’s racing record, which includes 140 wins, 405 podiums, 56 pole positions in 343 races, and five titles, such as the GT World Challenge Endurance Cup Pro class Drivers’ and Team titles, plus victories at the Nürburgring 24 Hours and Daytona 24 Hours. It also incorporates insights from the 296 LMGT3, set to compete in the FIA WEC from 2024. Developed with client feedback, the 296 GT3 Evo focuses on improved competitiveness across various conditions while maintaining the modular design of its predecessor, aiding maintenance and setup. The V6 combustion engine, with a 120° V configuration and turbochargers inside the V, remains unchanged, offering compactness, reduced weight, and strong power delivery. Positioned lower and further forward than in the road car, it lowers the center of gravity and enhances torsional rigidity. The engine is tilted 2° to accommodate a larger rear diffuser. The gearbox features a new gear ratio cascade, optimized based on two years of racing data to improve torque delivery at low and high speeds. The 296 GT3 Evo aims to balance performance, reliability, and ease of use for racing teams.

name: 296 Speciale A 2026
description: Ferrari introduced the 296 Speciale A, a mid-rear-engine plug-in hybrid spider, joining the lineage of exclusive berlinetta-derived spiders like the Scuderia Spider 16M, 458 Speciale A, and 488 Pista Spider. Unveiled as a high-performance convertible, it builds on the 296 GTS with a retractable hardtop, enhancing open-air driving while maintaining agile handling and stability. The 296 Speciale A’s plug-in hybrid system combines a 120° V6 twin-turbo engine and an electric motor, delivering 880 hp—50 hp more than the 296 GTS and a record for a rear-wheel-drive Ferrari. The V6, producing 700 cv, incorporates motorsport-derived features like titanium connecting rods, reinforced pistons, a lighter crankshaft, and Formula 1-inspired knock control. The electric motor, upgraded to 180 cv in extra boost mode, supports a revised eight-speed DCT transmission strategy that uses additional torque to reduce shift times. The engine’s sound emphasizes the 3rd, 6th, and 9th combustion orders, enhanced in quality and volume. Aerodynamic improvements, drawn from the 296 Challenge, generate 435 kg of downforce at 250 km/h with the roof up, 20% more than the 296 GTS. Features include an aero damper in the front bonnet, vertical fins with side wings on the rear bumper, and an active rear spoiler with a new actuator reducing transition time between configurations by 50% and adding a Medium Downforce setting for better high-speed stability. Weight is reduced by 50 kg compared to the 296 GTS, using carbon fiber for body parts and titanium for engine components, achieving a 1.69 kg/cv power-to-weight ratio. Enhanced electronic controls, revised suspension with a 5 mm lower ride height, and updated tires improve braking precision, reduce cornering roll by 13%, and ensure predictable handling at the limit.

name: SC40 2026
description: On October 17, 2025, Ferrari unveiled the SC40, a bespoke one-off model from its elite Special Projects program, designed exclusively for a single client who sought to blend the raw spirit of the iconic F40 with contemporary Ferrari innovation. This mid-rear-engined berlinetta draws its core architecture, chassis, and powertrain directly from the acclaimed 296 GTB, featuring a plug-in hybrid V6 setup that delivers a staggering 819 horsepower through a twin-turbocharged 3.0-liter engine paired with an axial-flux electric motor. As part of Ferrari's pinnacle of personalization, the SC40 represents the brand's commitment to crafting unique vehicles that transcend production lines, embodying the desires of discerning collectors while pushing the boundaries of automotive artistry. The SC40's design, helmed by Ferrari Styling Centre director Flavio Manzoni, pays homage to the legendary F40—unveiled in 1987 to celebrate Enzo Ferrari's 40th anniversary—without resorting to mere replication. Its name evokes the F40's legacy, while sharp, angular lines and squared volumes channel the original's muscular geometry, softened by fluid surface transitions for a distinctly modern silhouette. Key nods include the "SC40" script engraved in relief on the right upright of the fixed rear wing, reminiscent of the F40's basket-handle spoiler, alongside triangular carbon-fiber accents and large intercooler intakes that reinterpret classic NACA ducts and louvers. The exterior gleams in a bespoke SC40 White paint, complemented by custom wheels with brushed metal and black spokes, creating an industrial aesthetic that aligns with Ferrari's evolving design language seen in models like the F80. Under the hood, the SC40 inherits the 296 GTB's rear-wheel-drive prowess, promising blistering performance with seamless power delivery from its hybrid system, though exact acceleration figures remain under wraps for this singular creation. Aerodynamic enhancements, such as smoked Lexan louvers exposing the powertrain and a 3D-printed titanium-carbon exhaust exiting centrally, underscore its track-ready ethos, while the overall form emphasizes precision and motion through vertical structural lines. This fusion of nostalgia and cutting-edge engineering ensures the SC40 not only honors its predecessor but elevates it into a forward-looking supercar, capable of rivaling modern hypercars in both form and function. Inside, the cabin revives the F40's unapologetic intensity with premium materials like carbon-Kevlar in the footwells, floor mats, and steering wheel, paired with Charcoal Alcantara upholstery and red Jacquard accents for a sporty yet refined ambiance. Developed over approximately two years in close collaboration with the client, the SC40 exemplifies Ferrari's personalization philosophy, where every detail—from the negative-etched Ferrari logo revealing underlying carbon fiber to the assertive front-end with outer-mounted headlights—reflects tailored vision. While destined for private ownership, a styling buck of the SC40 will debut at the Ferrari Museum in Maranello starting October 18, 2025, allowing enthusiasts a glimpse into this exquisite tribute and Ferrari's ongoing mastery of one-of-one masterpieces.

name: California 2009
description: The Ferrari California, designated as Type F149, is a grand touring sports car produced by Italian automobile manufacturer Ferrari. Introduced in 2008, it is a two-door 2+2 hardtop convertible that initially came equipped with a naturally aspirated 4.3-litre V8 engine, positioned in a front-mid mounted, rear-wheel drive layout. In 2012, Ferrari enhanced the lineup with the California 30, a lighter and slightly more powerful variant. The evolution continued with the second generation, named the California T, launched in 2014 with a new twin-turbo 3.9-litre V8. The California revives the historic names of the late 1950s Ferrari 250 GT California Spyder and the 1960s 365 California, tying modern innovation with nostalgic allure. Originally positioned as an entry-level model, the California nonetheless debuted with many of Ferrari’s latest design innovations and a base price on par with the F430, Ferrari's then-flagship V8 sports car. The California marked a new direction for Ferrari as it introduced a fourth model range at the October 2008 Paris Motor Show. This new model range was characterized by its front-engine, eight-cylinder configuration, complementing the existing lineup of mid-engine and front-engine vehicles across the Ferrari range. The California continued to be a significant part of Ferrari's lineup until it was succeeded by the Portofino in 2018, marking an end to its production but not its legacy.

name: California T 2015
description: The Ferrari California T, or Type 149M, is an updated version of the original California, featuring a new turbocharged design that was unveiled online in February 2014 and debuted at the Geneva Motor Show the following month. The "T" stands for Turbo, reintroducing turbo technology not seen since the Ferrari F40. This model showcases a refreshed exterior with influences from the F12, including a new front fascia and horizontally aligned exhaust pipes at the rear, alongside a redesigned interior. Powered by a 3.9-liter twin-turbo V8 engine, the California T outputs 553 hp and 557 lb-ft of torque, paired with a 7-speed dual-clutch transmission and updated MagneRide suspension. It accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h (62 mph) in 3.6 seconds with a top speed of 315 km/h (196 mph). Innovations include a 15% reduction in emissions over its predecessor and minimized turbo lag through a variable boost management system. The California T was also one of the first Ferraris to incorporate Apple CarPlay, aligning advanced technology with luxury and performance in Ferrari’s grand touring lineup.

name: Portofino 2018
description: The Ferrari Portofino, designated as Type F164, is a grand touring sports car crafted by the renowned Italian automaker Ferrari. Introduced as the successor to the Ferrari California T, the Portofino is a luxurious 2+2 hardtop convertible that combines performance with style. It is equipped with a powerful 3.9 L twin-turbo V8 gasoline engine that enables the car to accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in just 3.5 seconds. Named after the picturesque Italian Riviera village of Portofino, the car epitomizes the elegance and allure associated with the coastal town. The Ferrari Portofino was officially unveiled at two exclusive events in the village of Portofino on September 7 and 8, 2017, with notable attendees including Piero Ferrari, Sergio Marchionne, Sebastian Vettel, and Giancarlo Fisichella. The unveiling was part of a broader celebration, as it also featured prominently during the Ferrari 70th Anniversary events held in Maranello on September 9 and 10. Further, the Portofino made its public debut at the 2017 Frankfurt Motor Show, marking its entry into the global market. Looking ahead, the legacy of the Portofino's design and mechanical underpinnings continues, as it served as the basis for the 2020 Ferrari Roma coupe. In a nod to evolving consumer preferences and the success of its design, a convertible version of the Roma was introduced in 2023, signaling the end of the Portofino's production but leaving a lasting impact on Ferrari's lineup of grand touring vehicles.

name: Portofino M 2021
description: On 16 September 2020, Car and Driver reported the launch of the Portofino M (Modificata or Modified). The power was increased to 612 hp and was released in the middle of 2021.

name: Roma 2022
description: The Ferrari Roma, designated as Type F169, is a grand touring car from the iconic Italian automaker Ferrari, drawing aesthetic inspiration from historical models like the Ferrari 250 GT Lusso and 250 GT 2+2. Introduced online on November 13, 2019, and publicly unveiled the following day in Rome, the car pays homage to Italy's capital, reflecting its name. The Roma boasts a front mid-engine, rear-wheel-drive configuration with a turbocharged V8 engine, positioned in Ferrari’s lineup between the Portofino and the F8 Tributo. It features a 2+2 seating arrangement, making it both sporty and somewhat family-friendly within the context of high-performance vehicles. In terms of design, the Roma received accolades such as the Red Dot award, thanks to its modern yet classic features which include flush door handles, slim LED lights, and an active rear spoiler that integrates seamlessly into the body when not in use. In 2023, Ferrari expanded the Roma’s appeal by introducing a soft-top convertible version. The interior of the Roma is distinguished by a "2+" setup, offering a small backseat area. It includes state-of-the-art digital instrumentation like a 16.0-inch wrap-around touch screen and a multi-function steering wheel, both shared with the SF90 Stradale. A trim piece elegantly separates the driver and passenger areas and blends into the dashboard, which houses an 8.4-inch portrait touch screen that controls numerous car functions. Additionally, an optional horizontal touchscreen on the passenger side allows for access to various controls and performance metrics. The car's sophistication extends to a newly designed key that simplifies entry with a button press near the flush door handles, enhancing both functionality and aesthetics.

name: Roma Tailor Made China 2023
description: Ferrari is commemorating its 30th anniversary in mainland China with the unveiling of a special, one-of-a-kind Tailor Made Ferrari Roma. This unique edition of the Roma celebrates Ferrari's three decades of presence in China since the first order was placed in Beijing in 1992. The Tailor Made Ferrari Roma embodies a fusion of traditional Chinese aesthetics and Italian design, featuring bespoke elements that highlight Ferrari's commitment to personalization through its Tailor Made programme. For this project, Ferrari collaborated with Jiang Qiong'er, a renowned Chinese designer known for her work that deeply respects and reflects traditional Chinese culture. Jiang's design philosophy brings subtlety, beauty, and heritage to the forefront, making her an ideal partner for Ferrari as they look to honor their significant milestones in China. The design of the Tailor Made Roma by Jiang Qiong'er and Ferrari integrates classic Chinese aesthetics with the sleek, contemporary lines of Italian automotive design, resulting in a vehicle that not only represents a bridge between cultures but also showcases the pinnacle of customized luxury. The collaboration emphasizes a blend of Chinoiserie Chic with Italian craftsmanship, celebrating Ferrari's ongoing journey and growth in the Chinese market as it enters its fourth decade.

name: Roma Spider 2024
description: In March 2023, Ferrari revealed the Roma Spider. It is meant to be the replacement for the Ferrari Portofino. Based on the successful Ferrari Roma, the new Spider variant features a soft top. This marks the return of a soft top for a front-engined Ferrari after 54 years since the 1969 365 GTS4.

name: Roma Spider Tailor Made 2024
description: The Ferrari Roma Spider Tailor Made, showcased at the Enzo Ferrari Museum in Modena as part of the "Ferrari One of a Kind" exhibition, highlights Ferrari’s personalization capabilities through its Tailor Made department. This unique convertible emphasizes innovative materials and production techniques. The seats feature manually stitched leather panels using thread from recycled tires. The aluminum body incorporates laser-etched components, including a front wing shield and a bonnet with a compass rose and Maranello’s geographical coordinates. Some V8 engine parts use recycled aluminum from Ferrari’s foundry, minimizing waste. The car also employs chrome-free nappa leather and sustainably sourced wood for the tonneau cover and rear floor. The center console includes components made from sand recycled from Maranello’s foundry casts. In collaboration with Montblanc, a special edition Meisterstück pen was created, featuring Oro Mida coloring matching the car, a handcrafted Au750 gold and platinum-coated nib with a compass motif, and platinum-coated cap and sections. The pen comes with a logbook covered in reused Rosso Bologna and Oro Mida metallic leather, embossed with Maranello’s coordinates, linking it to the car’s design. This Roma Spider serves as a platform for researching new materials and techniques, expanding Ferrari’s personalization options. The exhibition offers an immersive experience, showcasing materials, fabrics, and colors available through the Ferrari Styling Centre, with an interactive wall allowing visitors to explore customization possibilities. The car exemplifies Ferrari’s commitment to combining innovative craftsmanship with sustainability in its bespoke offerings. (Word count: 231)

name: Amalfi 2026
description: The Ferrari Amalfi, a front-mid-engine V8 2+ coupé, replaces the Ferrari Roma. It blends high performance with practicality, targeting drivers who want sporty dynamics without sacrificing daily usability. Its design features clean lines, a prominent front air intake, and a long bonnet housing a 640 hp twin-turbo V8 engine from the F154 family. The rear has an active spoiler for high-speed stability, complemented by forged wheels and carbon fiber accents. The interior uses a dual-cockpit layout with a new steering wheel featuring physical buttons and a start button. A 10.25" central display and ergonomic controls enhance driver interaction, while carbon fiber and contrasting stitching add a premium feel. The 2+ seating configuration increases cargo space, making it suitable for small trips or carrying extra gear. Performance includes a 0-100 km/h sprint in 3.3 seconds and 0-200 km/h in 9.0 seconds, with a power-to-weight ratio of 2.29 kg/hp. The eight-speed dual-clutch transmission delivers quick, smooth shifts. Driving dynamics are improved with a brake-by-wire system, an "ABS Evo" controller for varied conditions, and a recalibrated steering system for precise handling. Active aerodynamics, including a rear mobile wing, ensure stability across driving modes. Tech features include a connected infotainment system with Apple CarPlay® and Android Auto® as standard, plus wireless smartphone charging. The digital instrument cluster and central display create an intuitive interface. An optional front lifter system raises the car by 40 mm for urban obstacles, and a Burmester® audio system is available for enhanced sound. The Ferrari Amalfi balances performance and elegance, offering a versatile driving experience for both spirited drives and everyday use.

name: Amalfi Spider 2027
description: The Ferrari Amalfi Spider is a new 2+2 front-mid-engined V8 convertible that slots into Ferrari’s range as a more versatile and everyday-usable open-top model. It shares its core architecture with the Amalfi coupe but adds a fabric soft top, delivering a balance of sporty driving dynamics and grand-tourer comfort. Powered by an updated 3.855-litre twin-turbo V8 producing 640 hp at 7,500 rpm and 760 Nm of torque, the car reaches a top speed of 320 km/h and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in 3.3 seconds. It sits on a 2,670 mm wheelbase with a dry weight of 1,556 kg and uses an eight-speed dual-clutch transmission. The design, led by Flavio Manzoni’s Ferrari Design Studio, maintains the elegant, flowing lines of the coupe even with the roof down. The soft top, available in four tailor-made and two technical fabrics (including the new Tecnico Ottanio), opens in 13.5 seconds at speeds up to 60 km/h and stows in a compact 220 mm package. This preserves strong luggage capacity — 255 litres with the roof closed and 172 litres open. New details include the Rosso Tramonto paint option, a large front air intake, sculpted bonnet, and an active rear spoiler that adjusts for different downforce levels. Inside, the Amalfi Spider features a clean dual-cockpit layout with a strong emphasis on intuitive controls. It includes a new steering wheel with physical buttons, a prominent aluminium start button, and a trio of displays: a 15.6-inch digital instrument cluster, a 10.25-inch central touchscreen, and an 8.8-inch passenger screen. The 2+2 seating offers added practicality for occasional rear passengers or extra storage, while an integrated wind deflector reduces cabin turbulence when driving roof-down. Materials and trim can match the soft top fabric for visual continuity between exterior and interior. On the road, the car benefits from advanced vehicle dynamics including brake-by-wire, ABS Evo, and Side Slip Control 6.1. It rides on 20-inch wheels with 245/35 front and 285/35 rear tyres. Aerodynamic features such as underbody vortex generators, wheel fairings, and the multi-position active rear wing help maintain stability and efficiency. The five-layer acoustic soft top provides insulation close to that of a hard-top convertible, making it suitable for both spirited drives and comfortable long-distance journeys.

name: 308 GT4 1988-1993
description: The Dino 308 GT4, first unveiled at the Paris Motor Show in October 1973 with chassis number 07202, was initially the only example produced that year. It was painted in Azzurro Metallizzato (Light Blue Metallic) and represented a significant model for Ferrari, both for its design and branding strategy. Released as a 1974 model, the 308 GT4 underwent a significant branding change in May 1976 when Ferrari added the "Prancing Horse" badge, replacing the Dino badges on various parts of the car except for the rear boot lid, which retained the Dino 308GT4 logo. This rebranding caused confusion among owners, enthusiasts, and judges over the years due to the mixed identification signals on the car. The Dino name, a tribute to Enzo Ferrari's late son, was not as recognizable as the Ferrari brand, leading to hesitation among prospective buyers during the energy crisis when many were unsure about investing in a luxury car not explicitly badged as a "Ferrari." To boost sales and address these branding issues, Ferrari issued factory update #265/1 on July 1, 1975, which included technical and cosmetic revisions. These updates were inconsistently applied by dealers, resulting in a variety of modification levels across the 1975 GT4s, complicating the authentication process for Ferrari aficionados. Updates ranged from the addition of Prancing Horse badges and new paint schemes to mechanical adjustments like air conditioning improvements and exhaust modifications tailored for the North American market. Notably, 1975 was also significant for Ferrari as it marked Niki Lauda winning the Formula One drivers' championship and Ferrari securing the constructors' title. The GT4 holds the unique distinction of being the only 2+2 Ferrari to receive factory racing support. The GT4 model saw two series in its production run. The earlier series featured a twin distributor engine and fog lamps mounted on the front valance, while later models switched to a single distributor engine with fog lamps integrated behind the front grille, reflecting continual evolutions in design and functionality.

name: 208 GT4 1988-1993
description: The 208 GT4, launched at the Geneva Motor Show in 1975, was Ferrari’s strategic response to the Italian tax regulations that levied heavy duties on cars with engines over two liters. To accommodate this, Ferrari engineered the 208 GT4 with a de-bored engine measuring 66.8 by 71 mm, resulting in a 2.0 L (1991 cc) V8. This adaptation created the smallest production V8 engine ever for a road car, tailored specifically for the Italian market, where the tax on larger engines was more than double at 38%. This model produced 170 hp (125 kW) at 7700 rpm, achieving a top speed of 220 km/h (137 mph). The adjustments for the 208 GT4 weren't limited to engine size; it also featured smaller Weber 34 DCNF carburetors, a lower final drive ratio, and narrower tires to optimize performance within the constraints of its reduced engine size. Visually, the 208 GT4 was distinguished from its larger-engined counterparts by chrome accents instead of black on the exterior and the absence of fog lights. Internally, it sported a black dashboard facing as opposed to the silver typically found in other GT4 models. These design and technical tweaks made the 208 GT4 a unique entry in Ferrari's lineup, reflecting a tailored approach to meet specific market demands while maintaining the marque’s distinguished performance and style.

name: Mondial 8 1980-1982
description: The Mondial 8, unveiled at the 1980 Geneva Auto Salon, marked a significant departure for Ferrari as it moved away from the traditional 3-digit naming scheme. Despite its novelty, the Mondial 8 received a lukewarm reception from some critics who felt its performance was mild compared to other Ferrari models, sparking some criticism in the automotive press. This model featured a mid/rear-mounted V8 engine, which utilized the Bosch K-Jetronic fuel injection system, similar to that used in the 308 GTBi/GTSi. This engine, originally developed for the 1973 Dino 308 GT4, was mounted transversely and did not incorporate a computer, relying instead on mechanical components with just a few relays to manage the cold start sequence. The chassis of the Mondial 8 extended the 308 GT4's wheelbase by 100 mm to 2,650 mm (104.3 in), enhancing its stability and ride. The suspension setup maintained Ferrari’s classic configuration of unequal-length double wishbones paired with Koni dampers, ensuring responsive handling and a smooth drive despite the vehicle's somewhat underwhelming power output criticisms.

name: Mondial Quattrovalvole 1982-1985
description: Introduced in 1982, the Mondial Quattrovalvole represented an evolution in the Mondial series, incorporating significant enhancements to the engine. While the first Mondial featured a DOHC design with just two valves per cylinder, the Quattrovalvole, or QV, introduced a four-valve head, increasing efficiency and power. This new engine design drew inspiration from early eighties Formula 1 technology, underscoring Ferrari's commitment to transferring its racing pedigree to street vehicles. The engine, shared with the contemporary 308 GTB/GTS QV, produced 240 hp (177 kW). Aesthetically, the Mondial QV maintained the general appearance of the Mondial 8 but featured distinctive red engine heads and a prominent "Quattrovalvole" script at the rear, distinguishing it from its predecessor. Between 1982 and 1985, Ferrari produced 1,145 coupés, marking a successful production run that offered a blend of performance improvements while maintaining the model’s original design ethos.

name: Mondial QV Cabriolet 1983-1985
description: In 1983, Ferrari expanded the Mondial lineup by introducing a Cabriolet convertible model, adding a new dimension to the series. This model retained the same body styling as the coupe, including the distinctive 'buttress' design of the roof. However, to accommodate the convertible structure, the rear seats were positioned closer together laterally, slightly altering the interior layout. The launch of the Cabriolet significantly boosted the Mondial's popularity, especially in the American market, where the demand for convertibles was particularly strong. Notably, the Mondial Cabriolet stands out as the only four-seat, mid-rear engine convertible manufactured in regular production, a unique feature that added to its appeal. Between 1983 and 1985, only 629 units of this version were produced, making it the rarest iteration of the Mondial. This exclusivity, combined with its distinctive configuration, cemented its status as a highly sought-after model among collectors and enthusiasts.

name: Mondial 3.2 (Coupe + Cabriolet) 1985-1988
description: In 1985, the Mondial's engine was upgraded to match the enhancements seen in the new Ferrari 328. The engine's bore and stroke were increased, bringing its total displacement to 3.2 liters (3,185 cc) and boosting output to 270 hp (199 kW). This revised model, known as the Mondial 3.2, was unveiled at the Frankfurt Auto Show in September of that year. The Mondial 3.2 was available in both Coupé and Cabriolet variants, featuring a styling refresh that included restyled and body-colored bumpers that aligned more closely with the 328's design, integrating indicators and driving lamps more seamlessly. Additionally, it featured new alloy wheels with a more rounded face. The interior also received significant updates, with a more ergonomic layout and a redesigned, more rounded instrument binnacle that improved the overall driving experience. From 1987 onwards, later versions of the Mondial 3.2 included ABS brakes, enhancing the car's safety features. The fuel system continued to use the Bosch K-Jetronic (CIS) mechanical injection, augmented by an O2 sensor in the exhaust that provided feedback to a simple computer. This setup allowed for mixture adjustments through a pulse modulated frequency valve that regulated the control fuel pressure. The ignition system was managed by Marelli Microplex, featuring electronic advance control and dual distributors, one for each bank of the V8 engine. The 1988 model year marked the last iteration of the Mondial 3.2, notable for retaining the lower maintenance costs associated with the 308/328 drivetrain. This advantage allowed major service tasks, such as timing belt and clutch replacements, to be performed without removing the engine and transmission package from the vehicle, simplifying upkeep and reducing ownership costs.
