name: 499P Racecar 2023
description: Ferrari marked its return to top-level sports car racing after 50 years by participating in the 2023 FIA World Endurance Championship with the goal of achieving its first outright victory at Le Mans since 1966. The 499P, developed specifically for this competition, was designed in compliance with the Le Mans Hypercar regulations. This car features a carbon-fibre monocoque chassis, double wishbone suspension, and is powered by a twin-turbo V6 engine derived from the 296 GTB's three-liter engine, highlighted by its turbos mounted within the V configuration. The 499P also includes a hybrid electric motor on the front axle, which aligns with modern racing advancements. The vehicle was crafted to be highly reliable and to perform consistently within a broad aerodynamic range, necessary for endurance racing where Balance of Performance (BOP) regulations cap the performance. Ferrari's reentry into the sport was underscored by its distinctive red and yellow livery, reminiscent of the 312 PB used in 1973, and the racing numbers 50 and 51, symbolizing the years since its last participation. The team's lineup combined seasoned Ferrari GT drivers and ex-Formula 1 racer Antonio Giovanazzi. Debuting at the Sebring 1000 Miles, the 499P faced intense competition from major contenders like Toyota, Porsche, and Cadillac. Despite some initial technical challenges, the car proved its capability at Le Mans, where after an intense contest with Toyota, the #51 Ferrari clinched a celebrated victory at the race's centenary edition.

name: 499P Modificata 2024
description: Ferrari unveiled the 499P Modificata at the Mugello Circuit during the Ferrari World Finals, marking a significant celebration of its return to top-class endurance racing. This track-only car, not bound by FIA WEC regulations, is a high-performance evolution of the 499P, which recently claimed victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans. This model is part of a new direction for Ferrari—developing cars directly from racing models rather than production vehicles, allowing for advanced technical modifications. These enhancements include an electric axle and four-wheel drive capabilities at low speeds, the "Push to Pass" feature providing additional power, custom Pirelli tyres, and recalibrated suspension, electronic controls, and engine mappings. The 499P Modificata maintains the single-seater cockpit and ergonomics of the 499P, offering the same driving experience as the race-winning cars. It boasts a hybrid powertrain with a mid-rear mounted V6 engine and a front axle electric motor, collectively delivering 870 horsepower. This setup is coupled with a seven-speed sequential gearbox and incorporates an energy recovery system utilizing Ferrari's Formula 1 technology. Additionally, the car features a carbon-fibre monocoque chassis, advanced suspension, and braking systems to enhance driving performance and safety. The design is marked by its aerodynamic efficiency, evident in elements like the sculpted flanks, expressive headlights, and a distinctive rear double wing, which reflects Ferrari's iconic styling. Starting in 2024, the 499P Modificata will be a part of Ferrari's Sport Prototipi Clienti programme, offering owners exclusive track events with comprehensive support from Ferrari, including logistics, track-side assistance, and maintenance. This initiative mirrors the legacy of Ferrari's historical achievements in endurance racing, symbolizing a continuation of its celebrated motorsport lineage.

name: Ferrari Vision Gran Turismo 2022
description: The Ferrari Vision Gran Turismo is a high-performance concept car conceptualized by Flavio Manzoni, representing a bold leap forward in Ferrari's design language, both for road and racing applications. Ferrari describes the vehicle as a "futuristic design manifesto," blending advanced technology with pioneering aesthetics. At the heart of the Vision Gran Turismo is the V6 engine originally developed for the 499P Le Mans Hypercar, now unleashed to deliver an astonishing 1,030 PS (760 kW; 1,020 hp) at 9,000 rpm and 900 N⋅m (664 lb⋅ft) of torque at 5,500 rpm. This formidable internal combustion engine is complemented by three electric motors—two mounted at the front and one at the rear—providing the car with permanent all-wheel drive. The combined power output reaches a staggering 1,356 PS (1,337 hp; 997 kW) and 1,100 N⋅m (811 lb⋅ft), ensuring unparalleled performance. Power is transmitted to the rear wheels through an 8-speed dual-clutch transmission adapted from Ferrari’s Formula One program, ensuring that the Vision Gran Turismo marries its racetrack pedigree with road car sensibilities. Entry into the car's single seat cockpit is achieved through the windscreen, which doubles as a door, a novel feature reminiscent of the Lamborghini Egoista. The vehicle also boasts several advanced aerodynamic components, including a patented design that efficiently channels air around the cockpit to the sides, enhancing aerodynamic efficiency and stability at high speeds. The rear wing and diffuser draw inspiration from the 499P, while stylistic echoes of the Ferrari Modulo concept and the Ferrari 330 P3 integrate Ferrari’s historical design elements into a forward-thinking prototype. Ferrari's aspirations for this concept are underscored by its projected performance metrics, with the company claiming it can lap the Fiorano Circuit in under 1 minute and 10 seconds. This time is nearly ten seconds quicker than that of Ferrari’s current flagship road car, the SF90, setting a new benchmark in Ferrari's performance standards.

name: Testa Rossa J 2021
description: The Ferrari Testa Rossa J is a unique and limited edition project crafted to allow enthusiasts of all ages to experience the thrill of driving a legendary vehicle. This 75% scale replica of the iconic 1957 250 Testa Rossa is powered by an electric engine and mirrors the original classic in detail and spirit. Limited to just 299 units, this special model is designed for passionate collectors and is not homologated for road use. The collaboration between Ferrari and The Little Car Company ensures that every aspect of the Testa Rossa J's creation is meticulously handled, from its design overseen by Ferrari’s Centro Stile in Maranello to the chassis components derived from Ferrari’s original Classiche department drawings. The vehicle is suitable for drivers over 14 years of age, making it a family-friendly model that brings the illustrious Ferrari heritage into the hands of the next generation. Detailing the illustrious history of its full-sized counterpart, the 250 Testa Rossa was a dominant force on racetracks during the 1950s and 1960s, securing multiple victories and championships, and is the only Ferrari to win the 24 Hours of Le Mans four times. In terms of design, the Testa Rossa J showcases hand-beaten aluminum bodywork and employs the same high-quality paint used in Ferrari’s current road car lineup. Its chassis is digitally recreated from the original blueprints to ensure authenticity in handling and appearance. The interior is equally refined, featuring high-quality leather and a single seat design that can accommodate an adult and a teen, with classic instrumentation adapted for electric driving. Performance-wise, the Testa Rossa J is equipped with three batteries that offer a range of about 90 km and features a variety of driving modes to cater to different skill levels, from Novice to Race mode. Each mode adjusts the car’s power output and speed to ensure a safe yet exhilarating driving experience. Safety is paramount in the Testa Rossa J, with reinforced metal side panels, optional roll-bar, and modern Brembo disc brakes. The car maintains the aesthetic of the original with contemporary safety enhancements, combining historical allure with modern technology. This blend of classic Ferrari design and innovative engineering makes the Testa Rossa J a remarkable tribute to its racing heritage and a coveted piece for any collector.

name: PACCO GARA 2021
description: Introducing “Pacco Gara” the all new race pack now available for the Ferrari Testa Rossa J, featuring: Performance enhancement increasing power to 14kw, a quicker ratio steering rack, adjustable dampers, drilled brake discs and brake bias, all creating a more responsive and track-focused car. A bolt-on, removable roll cage, offering clients extra protection for those drivers wishing to enjoy their Testa Rossa J in racing environments. An additional exterior mirror has been fitted to the driver’s side, along with period-correct spotlights. A tonneau cover over the passenger side has also been incorporated, in the same Ferrari leather as the interior of the car.

name: F50 Bolide 1996
description: Custom one-off commissioned by the Sultan of Brunei.
